g.text: Recursion Schemes — separate shape from recursion
cata alg = alg . fmap (cata alg) . unFix
ana coalg = Fix . fmap (ana coalg) . coalg
hylo alg coalg = alg . fmap (hylo alg coalg) . coalg

div.p: Base functor F: the shape of one level of the data type, with type parameter R in place of every recursive child.
Fix F: the fixed point — Fix F = F (Fix F) — recovers the full recursive type from the shape alone.
fmap: apply a function to every R inside F R. This is the only Functor instance needed.
Once you have fmap, you get cata, ana, hylo for free.

g.title: Recurse on all children first (bottom-up). Each child Fix F is reduced to a.
Apply alg once at the current node. alg never recurses — it only sees F a.
Produce one layer of structure F a from seed a.
Recurse into each seed to build the full subtree.
hylo = cata . ana but fused. No Fix is ever allocated — the intermediate structure never exists.
